name: Regeneron Science Talent Search
amount: Top prize $250,000; finalist awards from $25,000; semifinalist awards $2,000

eligibility.citizenship: U.S. citizen or permanent resident; must be enrolled in a U.S. high school
eligibility.other_requirements: Must be a high school senior; must submit an independent research project with scientific merit; project may be in any field of science, math, or engineering

stackable_notes: Prize money (not a traditional scholarship) — awarded as a prize for research achievement. Generally not considered institutional scholarship income; most colleges do not reduce aid for prize winnings. Verify with individual institutions.
notes: Formerly known as the Westinghouse and Intel Science Talent Search — the oldest and most prestigious pre-college science competition in the U.S. Note: this is a competition prize, not a traditional scholarship application. Semifinalists (300) each receive $2,000; 40 finalists travel to Washington D.C. and compete for awards up to $250,000. The application itself doubles as a college-level research portfolio that strengthens admissions.
